design_philosophy.core_loop: Possess household objects via colored 'Fright 'ems' indicators to scare family members out of rooms; deplete their fear meters to complete houses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (ecto energy depletion over time) vs. family fear accumulation; risk-reward of possession duration
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mischievous ghost poltergeist wreaking havoc through everyday objects; comedy-horror revenge narrative against yuppie family
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game prioritizes environmental interaction and object possession mechanics over direct ghost combat. Emphasizes creative scare choreography (three Fright 'em types with distinct activation patterns) rather than reflexive action. Revenge/punk attitude defines protagonist and narrative framing—Polterguy is sympathetic vandal, not generic villain.

difficulty.lives_system: Health portrait in Underworld stages that shrinks with damage; when depleted, game over. Portrait does not replenish between Underworld stages except rare star-shaped ecto drops for full heal
difficulty.checkpointing: Underworld dungeon stages serve as recovery intervals when ecto depletes during house stages; exit point in underworld returns Polterguy to exact house location
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player chooses which objects to possess and when to trigger scares (especially manual orange Fright 'ems); can manage ecto depletion rate through strategic use of spells; no difficulty settings documented

progression.upgrade_system: Spells found in Underworld stages that provide ecto recovery or effects; approximately 400 interactive objects across four houses with escalating complexity
progression.unlock_mechanism: Must scare all family members out of each house to advance to next; four houses total before facing Ecto Beast boss
replayability_hooks: Two-player turn-based mode with simultaneous Underworld competition
Multiple possession strategies for each room (blue/orange/green Fright 'em types allow different approach patterns)
Twelve dungeon paths that repeat after completion—procedural variation in Underworld recovery stages

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through yuppie family home design across four progressively different houses
Character motivation established via opening premise: punk teenager killed by mogul's defective skateboards, now haunting his family
Protagonist personality reinforced through gameplay (vandalism/chaos as core mechanic)
Family dog transforms into grotesque Ecto Beast—visual narrative escalation

boss_design.philosophy: Family dog 'Ecto Beast' transforms into grotesque monster after each house cleared; serves as capstone encounter and visual narrative climax of family exodus
notable_bosses: Ecto Beast (transforms after each of four houses; confrontation between house completion and next house)

faction_indicators: Sardini family (yuppie mogul family, capitalist antagonists)
Vito Sardini (business mogul responsible for Polterguy's death via product negligence)
danger_signals: Dog barks drain ecto energy—family pet weaponized against protagonist
Ecto Beast emergence after family flees—escalating supernatural threat
Underworld hazards (bats, skulls, disembodied hands, steam vents)—environmental hostility increases
environmental_storytelling: Four progressively different house designs indicating family wealth/status
Household objects available for possession reflect room function and family lifestyle
Underworld dungeon aesthetic contrasts with domestic house interiors—visual geography of ghost's power
